stories.title: US Open Round 3 — Clark's Lead Tested at Shinnecock
stories.angle: Round 3 of the 126th US Open is underway at Shinnecock Hills Golf Club. Wyndham Clark entered Saturday at -7, 4 shots clear of a pack at -3 (Matt Fitzpatrick, Xander Schauffele, Sam Stevens, Tom Kim). Clark's opening 64 set the Shinnecock 36-hole record. The final pairing of Clark and Fitzpatrick teed off at 3:45 PM ET. McIlroy (even) and Scheffler (+1) sit 7-8 shots back but both made the cut and remain in mathematical contention. Notable missed cuts: Bryson DeChambeau, Viktor Hovland, Jon Rahm, Shane Lowry.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Wyndham Clark enters Round 3 of the US Open at -7, 4 shots clear of the field. His opening 64 set the 36-hole Shinnecock record. Moving day at the 126th US Open is underway. Can anyone catch him? 🏆

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: US Open Saturday final pairing: Clark (-7) and Fitzpatrick (-3) off at 3:45 PM ET. McIlroy and Scheffler are 7-8 shots back but both made the cut. Shinnecock is unforgiving — anything can happen. #USOpen

article.headline: US Open Round 3: Wyndham Clark Leads by Four at Shinnecock as Moving Day Begins

stories.title: Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid — The World No. 1 and the Missing Major
stories.angle: Scottie Scheffler has won the Masters, the PGA Championship, and The Open Championship. The US Open is the one piece missing from a career Grand Slam. At Shinnecock Hills — the hardest course the USGA runs the Open on — Scheffler grinded out rounds despite trailing Clark by 7-8 shots. He played down the Grand Slam motivation publicly ("it's never been a motivating factor for me"), but the historical significance of this week is undeniable. A Scheffler win at Shinnecock would complete the set.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Scottie Scheffler has the Masters. The PGA Championship. The Open Championship. The US Open at Shinnecock Hills is the one missing. He's 8 shots back through 36 holes — but he's still here. #USOpen

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: "The Grand Slam has never been a motivating factor for me." — Scottie Scheffler, before the US Open. Whether he says it out loud or not, history is on the line at Shinnecock this weekend. #USOpen

article.headline: The Missing Major: Scottie Scheffler's Grand Slam Bid at Shinnecock Hills

stories.title: KPMG Women's PGA Championship Preview — Korda Eyes Third Straight Major
stories.angle: The KPMG Women's PGA Championship begins Thursday June 25 at Hazeltine National Golf Club in Chaska, Minnesota. $12 million purse. Nelly Korda (World No. 1) won the 2026 US Women's Open in early June and is now targeting her third straight major. She was the 2021 KPMG champion — she knows this course. Hazeltine hosted the 2016 Ryder Cup and is a classic Minnesota championship layout.

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text: Nelly Korda won the US Women's Open 3 weeks ago. Now she's targeting the KPMG Women's PGA Championship (June 25-28 at Hazeltine). She won this event in 2021. Three straight majors? It's possible. #LPGA

article.headline: KPMG Women's PGA Preview: Can Nelly Korda Win Three Straight Majors at Hazeltine?

stories.title: LIV Golf Louisiana Cancellation — A Signal for the Tour's Future?
stories.angle: LIV Golf's Louisiana event in New Orleans — scheduled for June 25-28 at City Park — has been cancelled/postponed. Louisiana paid $3.2 million under the contract; LIV is refunding most of it but keeping $2 million already spent on City Park upgrades. LIV CEO Scott O'Neil gave unclear answers this week about remaining 2026 tour stops. Jon Rahm leads LIV standings (928.58 points) with Bryson DeChambeau second (644.40). Tyrrell Hatton won LIV Andalucía in early June.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: LIV Golf's Louisiana event (New Orleans, June 25-28) has been cancelled. Louisiana paid $3.2M under the contract. LIV's CEO gave unclear answers this week about remaining 2026 tour stops. The questions keep growing. #LIVGolf

stories.title: Meijer LPGA Classic — Yan Liu Leads, First Tour Win on the Line
stories.angle: The Meijer LPGA Classic for Simply Give at Blythefield Country Club in Michigan is in its third round today (June 18-21). Yan Liu, winless on the LPGA Tour, shot a 6-under 66 to lead after Round 2. Tournament continues today. The event has a rich history as one of the LPGA's fan-favorite stops in Michigan.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Yan Liu leads the Meijer LPGA Classic after Round 2 with a 6-under 66. She's still searching for her first LPGA Tour win. Round 3 is underway today in Michigan. 🏌️‍♀️ #LPGA
